# UsageDeck 0.6.0

This release makes UsageDeck easier to set up on a new PC, improves notification control and reliability, and keeps provider and update information more dependable.

## Added

- Complete a guided first-run setup that finds supported providers already installed on your PC and lets you choose providers, theme, and notifications before the first refresh.

## Improvements

- Configure alerts separately for each provider and verify Windows notification delivery from a simpler Notifications page. Existing notification choices are copied to every provider automatically when upgrading.
- Detect Codex, Claude Code, Antigravity, GitHub CLI, Kiro, Amp, and OpenCode in their standard Windows installation locations even when UsageDeck starts with an outdated PATH.
- Keep long-running tray sessions aware of new releases with automatic update checks every six hours, in addition to the check at startup.
- Show Antigravity reset countdowns when the CLI reports a duration using “Refreshes in”.

## Fixes

- Fresh installations include the required .NET and Microsoft Windows components, improving notification reliability without separate runtime installation. Startup failures now show recovery guidance and save a privacy-safe diagnostic report.
- Notification controls now remain readable and save changes against the correct provider, while settings confirmation and error messages dismiss automatically.

The Windows installer and portable ZIP are larger because they include the required .NET and Microsoft Windows runtime components.
